sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.exact_document_title: Depression in adults: treatment and management — Recommendations
sources.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE mental-health, crisis, referral, and legal adaptation
sources.population: Adults with possible or diagnosed depression; communication and carer involvement are qualified.
sources.clinical_setting: Primary and mental healthcare recognition and assessment.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for documenting mood and interest screening, duration, course, function, social and interpersonal context, sleep, substances, medicines, loneliness and clinician-completed risk assessment. It does not diagnose depression or generate a referral or treatment.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.2.7 — recognition, comprehensive assessment, function, and context
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting low mood, loss of interest, duration, course, functional, interpersonal and social difficulty, previous mood history, physical and mental comorbidity, sleep, stressful events, medicines, substances, loneliness and support.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2.8–1.2.12 — clinician-completed risk assessment
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports recording that suicidal ideation, intent, support and immediate risk were directly assessed by a competent clinician; it does not populate a negative, assign risk, or generate escalation.

archive.title: Depression in adults: treatment and management — Recommendations
archive.publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
archive.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE mental-health, crisis, referral, and legal adaptation
archive.population: Adults with possible or diagnosed depression; communication and carer involvement are qualified.
archive.setting: Primary and mental healthcare recognition and assessment.

exact_relevant_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.2.7 — recognition, comprehensive assessment, function, and context
exact_relevant_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting low mood, loss of interest, duration, course, functional, interpersonal and social difficulty, previous mood history, physical and mental comorbidity, sleep, stressful events, medicines, substances, loneliness and support.

exact_relevant_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2.8–1.2.12 — clinician-completed risk assessment
exact_relevant_sections.evidence_summary: Supports recording that suicidal ideation, intent, support and immediate risk were directly assessed by a competent clinician; it does not populate a negative, assign risk, or generate escalation.

sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.exact_document_title: Generalised anxiety disorder and panic disorder in adults: management — Recommendations
sources.population: Adults with known or suspected generalised anxiety disorder or panic disorder.
sources.clinical_setting: Primary, community, and specialist mental-health care.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for adult GAD identification, assessment, stepped care, risk, and clinician-led management; not exact for children, every anxiety subtype, or UAE pathways.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2.2–1.2.11 — identification, assessment, education, and monitoring
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports anxiety and worry history, duration, distress, functional impairment, comorbid depression or anxiety, substance use, medical and treatment history, education, and active monitoring.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2.17–1.2.33 and 1.2.44–1.2.48 — clinician-led treatment and shared decisions
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-documented discussion of psychological or medication options, patient preference, treatment information, monitoring, and referral or relapse planning without autonomous treatment output.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2.37–1.2.39 — high risk and specialist assessment
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting severe functional impairment, self-harm or suicide risk, substance misuse, self-neglect, treatment history, home and community support, family impact, referral, and collaborative care planning.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.3.1–1.3.5 — diagnosis, presentation, and exclusion of acute physical problems
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports structured adult panic-disorder diagnosis, history, comorbidity review, panic presentations, and proportionate investigation to exclude acute physical problems before primary-care follow-up.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.3.6–1.3.25 — clinician-led treatment and shared decisions
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-documented discussion of psychological and medication options, patient preference, information, monitoring, self-help, referral, and follow-up without autonomous treatment output or doses.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.3.26–1.3.33 — reassessment, risk, and specialist care
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports reassessment of prior treatment and concordance, substance use, comorbidity, function, social support, stressors, avoidance, comprehensive risk, side effects, efficacy, and a shared care plan.
